title: Customer Churn Prediction and Early Warning
dataSources: **Product telemetry (customer-defined sourcetypes):** `index=app_events` with `sourcetype=app:login` and `sourcetype=app:feature_use` ingested through Splunk **HTTP Event Collector** — Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector → global enable + per-token stanza (example token display name `pa_churn_telemetry`, indexes `app_events`, ISO-8601 timestamps). Events may originate from first-party app instrumentation (Node `@splunk/logging`, Python `splunk_handler`, Go exporters), forwarded JSON from Mixpanel/Amplitude/Pendo/Heap-style pipelines renamed at ingest to `web:event`, or Salesforce Marketing Cloud Journey Builder firing into HEC; **`app:*` names are deployment conventions**, not Splunkbase-canonical sourcetypes.**Billing health:** `index=business` `sourcetype=dbx:billing` from **Splunk DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) JDBC inputs polling Stripe-backed Snowflake tables, Zuora/REST staging schemas, Recurly warehouse projections, NetSuite SuiteAnalytics Connect, or SAP billing extracts — scheduled input examples: `db_input=billing_daily_customer_health`, `db_input=znr_contract_payment_facts`. Expected columns include `customer_id`, `payment_status`, `days_past_due`, `failed_charge_count`, `last_payment_date`.**Support friction:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:incident` from **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) incident pull inputs; **or** Zendesk/Freshdesk tickets shipped via HEC (`zendesk:ticket`, `freshdesk:ticket`) — sourcetype varies by toolchain.**Key alignment:** optional `lookup` `customer_id_map.csv` with at least `raw_customer_id`, `canonical_customer_id` (and optional `crm_id`, `billing_id`) so Salesforce 15/18-char IDs, numeric billing keys, and ServiceNow references roll up to one churn grain.
app: **HTTP Event Collector** (native Splunk Enterprise / Cloud ingestion — no separate Splunkbase app; configure `inputs.conf` `[http://...]` stanzas and tokens in Splunk Web) for `app:login` / `app:feature_use` product analytics; **Splunk DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) for `dbx:billing` scheduled DB inputs; **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) when production support data lands as `snow:incident`. Mention **HEC** explicitly in deployment docs — tokens are created under Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector.
spl:
index=app_events sourcetype="app:login" earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
| eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, customer_key, crm_account_id)
| lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
| eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
| stats count AS logins_90d, latest(_time) AS last_login BY customer_id
| eval days_since_login=round((now()-last_login)/86400,0)
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=app_events sourcetype="app:feature_use" earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, customer_key, crm_account_id)
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
    | stats count AS feature_events_90d, dc(feature_name) AS features_used BY customer_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:incident" OR sourcetype="zendesk:ticket") earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, u_customer_id, account_number, caller_account)
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
    | eval pri_hi=if(match(lower(tostring(priority)), "^(1|2|high|critical|p1|p2)$") OR tonumber(priority)<=2, 1, 0)
    | stats count AS tickets_90d, sum(pri_hi) AS hi_pri_ticket_rows BY customer_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:billing" earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, billing_customer_id, netsuite_entity_id)
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
    | stats sum(eval(if(isnotnull(days_past_due) AND days_past_due>=7, 1, if(lower(payment_status) IN ("past_due","failed","charge_failed","writeoff_pending"), 1, 0)))) AS severe_payment_events BY customer_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 feature_events_90d features_used tickets_90d hi_pri_ticket_rows severe_payment_events
| eval churn_score_base=0
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(days_since_login > 30, 25, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(logins_90d < 10, 20, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(features_used < 3, 15, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(tickets_90d > 5, 20, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(severe_payment_events > 0, 20, 0)
| eval risk_decay_bonus=case(days_since_login<=3, -15, days_since_login<=7, -10, days_since_login<=14, -5, 1=1, 0)
| eval churn_score=max(0, churn_score_base + risk_decay_bonus)
| eval risk_level=case(churn_score>=60, "HIGH", churn_score>=30, "MEDIUM", 1=1, "LOW")
| where churn_score >= 30
| sort - churn_score
| table customer_id, risk_level, churn_score, churn_score_base, risk_decay_bonus, days_since_login, logins_90d, features_used, tickets_90d, severe_payment_events
description: Fuses **ninety-day** login cadence, **distinct feature breadth**, **support-incident volume**, and **billing stress** (severe late or failed-payment indicators) after mapping heterogeneous IDs through **`customer_id_map.csv`**, then applies a transparent additive **25 / 20 / 15 / 20 / 20** weight stack plus a **risk_decay_bonus** when recent logins prove re-engagement. **`join`** legs run with **`max=0`** so Splunk never silently truncates high-cardinality accounts at the default fifty-thousand join limit; billing gates count **`days_past_due>=7`** (or equivalent status tokens) so short grace-window noise does not dominate finance-led churn signals.
value: Customer Success Managers get a daily **watchlist ranked by composite churn_score** — answering "which subscription accounts deserve proactive outreach this week" without waiting for an unpaid invoice or a cancellation ticket. Retention economics typically run **five to twenty-five times cheaper than acquisition**, while acting too late wastes ARR and burns team credibility; noisy outreach driven only by billing quirks wastes CSM capacity and trains customers to ignore alerts. Pairing Splunk-side scoring with CRM ownership clarifies whether leadership needs portfolio staffing, finance negotiation, or product remediation.
implementation: Create **HEC tokens** (`pa_churn_telemetry`) for login/feature streams into `index=app_events`; schedule **DB Connect** pulls for **`dbx:billing`** into `index=business`; land ServiceNow **`snow:incident`** (1928) or HEC Zendesk/Freshdesk into `index=itsm`; publish **`customer_id_map.csv`**; paste the **`spl`** into a scheduled saved search (`*/30 * * * *` watchlist refresh), attach webhook or digest actions, and optionally **`collect`** HIGH transitions into **`summary index=churn_watchlist`** for ITSI Glass Tables.
visualization: **Column chart** (stacked): HIGH vs MEDIUM vs LOW account counts by ISO week for twelve rolling weeks. **Line chart**: twelve-week trend of median **churn_score** in the HIGH bucket. **Table** (heat-mapped **churn_score**): top thirty **`customer_id`** rows with drilldown to **`timeline`** visualization of **`app:login`**/**`app:feature_use`** alongside **`snow:incident`** markers. **Single value**: sum of **ARR** joined from **`dbx:subscriptions`** or CRM snapshot for rows where **`risk_level=HIGH`** (requires companion lookup or secondary panel search). **Scatter chart**: **`logins_90d`** vs **`features_used`** colored by **`risk_level`** to spot narrow-but-noisy accounts.

references.title: Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector
Splunk DB Connect — Deploy and use

knownFalsePositives: **Seasonal contract exits** — fixed-scope engagements conclude on schedule so login trails evaporate without billing distress.**SKU migrations** — leadership directs adoption onto a successor offering while legacy telemetry flatlines though wallet share persists.**Finance-led mergers** — acquiring firms consolidate billing identities overnight producing simultaneous churn-and-add pseudo-motion.**CRM duplicate remediation** — duplicate merges rewrite identifiers yielding fictitious churn bursts.**Quarter-close timezone seams** — ninety-day fences anchored UTC versus Pacific slip engagement tiers one sidereal day.**Instrumentation pivots** — new structured logging inflates feature breadth without habits improving.**Enterprise pilots without invoicing** — procurement extends evaluations while billing extracts stay silent despite heavy traffic.**Portfolio sunsets at holding-company level** — child logos churn while enterprise ARR grows elsewhere.**Marketing-led login spikes** — bursts suppress staleness penalties hiding dormant cohorts.**Collections courtesy credits** — goodwill adjustments erase severe payment flags yet sentiment stays brittle.**Warehouse latency** — nightly billing snapshots omit intra-day retries clearing balances.**Alternate ticketing channels** — email-only disputes bypass incident ingestion.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Heavy Forwarder or indexer-tier host permitted to hold JDBC secrets for **Splunk DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) and reachable JDBC endpoints (Snowflake, NetSuite SuiteAnalytics Connect, Zuora transactional replicas, SAP HANA).
• Splunk **HTTP Event Collector** enabled cluster-wide (`disabled=0` under `[http]` and REST ACLs unlocked per policy) plus TLS-terminated endpoints matching your Cloud stack or Enterprise indexer VIP.
• Optional **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) on a Heavy Forwarder pulling **`snow:incident`** into **`index=itsm`** with normalized priority fields.
• **`customer_id_map.csv`** stored under **`lookups/`** with **`Lookup Definitions`** referencing **`canonical_customer_id`** as the analytical key.
• Roles granting **`srchIndexesAllowed`** on **`app_events`**, **`business`**, **`itsm`**, **`summary`** (if collecting watchlist snapshots).

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
**Product telemetry via HEC:** In Splunk Web navigate **Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector → Global Settings**, enable HTTP Event Collector, note port **`8088`** (default). Click **New Token**, name it **`pa_churn_telemetry`**, pick **`index=app_events`**, default sourcetype **`app:login`** for the first token — duplicate tokens if you prefer distinct sourcetypes per stream (`app:feature_use`). Require acknowledgement if clients retry large batches.

Indexer-side stanza pattern (`etc/apps/pa_churn_hec/local/inputs.conf`):

```ini
[http://pa_churn_telemetry_login]
disabled = 0
indexes = app_events
index = app_events
token = <paste-token-uuid>
sourcetype = app:login
useACK = 1
```

Create a sibling stanza toggling **`sourcetype = app:feature_use`** when you split login vs workflow events. Set **Indexes** retention: **`app_events` 90 days** (covers the scoring window plus buffer) and **`business` 365 days** so seasonally billed customers retain year-ago comparisons. **Time parsing:** configure **JSON**/`props.conf` so `_time` honors ISO-8601 event times for product actions, not indexer arrival lag — late-arriving batches otherwise look like faux disengagement.

Instrument the product: embed the official **Node** (`@splunk/logging`), **Python** (`splunk_handler`, `requests.post` with HEC headers), or **OpenTelemetry** exporter that targets HEC JSON. Emit one JSON object per login with **`customer_id`**, **`user_id`**, **`login_method`**, **`mfa`**, and authoritative client clock. Emit one event per materially significant product action with **`feature_name`**, **`feature_category`**, **`customer_id`**, **`user_id`**. If analytics already live in Mixpanel/Amplitude/Pendo/Heap, schedule a lightweight forwarder job that pulls daily cohort files and rewrites them to `index=app_events` with **`sourcetype=web:event`** until native HEC instrumentation ships — document the bridge in the ingestion charter so counts stay comparable quarter over quarter.

**Billing via DB Connect:** Launch **Splunk DB Connect → Connections → New Connection**, choose JDBC driver (**Snowflake**, **NetSuite**, **PostgreSQL**, etc.), paste JDBC URL, store credentials via DB Connect credential vault. Under **Data Inputs → DB Inputs**, craft SQL aligned to subscription economics — template skeleton:

```sql
SELECT customer_id,
       payment_status,
       days_past_due,
       failed_charge_count,
       last_payment_date
FROM finance.customer_payment_facts
WHERE snapshot_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 120
```

Map output sourcetype **`dbx:billing`**, destination **`index=business`**, cron **`0 3 * * *`** in the subscriber's timezone so nightly warehouse loads finish before CSM morning standups. Stripe-centric estates often hydrate **`payment_status`** through Snowflake **`stripe.payment_intent`** replicas; Zuora-heavy stacks expose **`balance`** tables — tune **`severe`** predicates accordingly.

**Support ingestion:** Prefer **`snow:incident`** via TA-ServiceNow modular inputs configured with OAuth or mutual TLS per Splunk Secure Gateway guidance; alternative stacks ship **`zendesk:ticket`** JSON lines through dedicated HEC tokens with **`index=itsm`** for parity.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Paste the authoritative SPL block:

```spl
index=app_events sourcetype="app:login" earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
| eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, customer_key, crm_account_id)
| lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
| eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
| stats count AS logins_90d, latest(_time) AS last_login BY customer_id
| eval days_since_login=round((now()-last_login)/86400,0)
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=app_events sourcetype="app:feature_use" earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, customer_key, crm_account_id)
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
    | stats count AS feature_events_90d, dc(feature_name) AS features_used BY customer_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:incident" OR sourcetype="zendesk:ticket") earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, u_customer_id, account_number, caller_account)
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
    | eval pri_hi=if(match(lower(tostring(priority)), "^(1|2|high|critical|p1|p2)$") OR tonumber(priority)<=2, 1, 0)
    | stats count AS tickets_90d, sum(pri_hi) AS hi_pri_ticket_rows BY customer_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:billing" earliest=-90d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_customer_id=coalesce(customer_id, billing_customer_id, netsuite_entity_id)
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_customer_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_customer_id)
    | stats sum(eval(if(isnotnull(days_past_due) AND days_past_due>=7, 1, if(lower(payment_status) IN ("past_due","failed","charge_failed","writeoff_pending"), 1, 0)))) AS severe_payment_events BY customer_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 feature_events_90d features_used tickets_90d hi_pri_ticket_rows severe_payment_events
| eval churn_score_base=0
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(days_since_login > 30, 25, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(logins_90d < 10, 20, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(features_used < 3, 15, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(tickets_90d > 5, 20, 0)
| eval churn_score_base=churn_score_base + if(severe_payment_events > 0, 20, 0)
| eval risk_decay_bonus=case(days_since_login<=3, -15, days_since_login<=7, -10, days_since_login<=14, -5, 1=1, 0)
| eval churn_score=max(0, churn_score_base + risk_decay_bonus)
| eval risk_level=case(churn_score>=60, "HIGH", churn_score>=30, "MEDIUM", 1=1, "LOW")
| where churn_score >= 30
| sort - churn_score
| table customer_id, risk_level, churn_score, churn_score_base, risk_decay_bonus, days_since_login, logins_90d, features_used, tickets_90d, severe_payment_events
```

Stage-by-stage intent without repeating templated filler: the **`lookup`** pass translates Salesforce-style IDs into **`canonical_customer_id`** before **`stats`** — otherwise merges silently duplicate rows or orphan billing joins; the **`stats`** pass over **`app:login`** counts rolling interactions and derives **`days_since_login`** — leadership cares because dormant identities precede SaaS cancellations weeks before invoices fail; the **`feature_use`** join captures breadth — fewer than three distinct **`feature_name`** values implies shallow adoption modules worth addressing in success plays; **`snow:incident`** aggregates quantify reactive friction — sustained volumes above five often correlate with renewal brinkmanship independent of product bugs; **`dbx:billing`** aggregates elevate severity only when **`days_past_due≥7`** or explicit failure statuses appear — aligning collectors with finance's escalation policies avoids noisy pseudo-churn from grace clocks; **`risk_decay_bonus`** subtracts points when **`days_since_login`** falls inside a fourteen-day revitalization corridor — stale highs tied only to forty-day-old spikes decay naturally when telemetry proves renewed cadence; **`where churn_score≥30`** trims healthy logos so CS Ops receives actionable slices without pager saturation.

Weight rationale (**25 / 20 / 15 / 20 / 20**): staleness carries the highest single penalty because SaaS cancellations trail behavioral abandonment; login counts capture habitual rhythm; breadth exposes sticky workflows vs occasional tourists; incident spikes proxy unresolved friction; billing captures irrevocable finance escalation — collectively hitting executive escalation thresholds near score sixty. Tune quarterly using logistic regression on labeled churn cohorts exported from CRM **`Lost`** reasons versus **`Renewed`** cohorts.

Scheduling: **`*/30 * * * *`** intra-day refresh with **`earliest=-90d@d`** anchored for fence-post parity; nightly **`04:00`** digest summarizing deltas for VP Customer Success.

Alert actions: webhook into Salesforce Apex endpoints, Microsoft Teams adaptive cards for CSM pods, **`collect`/`summary`** index for ITSI downstream, or low-noise email routes — throttle **`customer_id`** to avoid duplicate outreach.

**Step 3 — Validate**
Pick five **`risk_level=HIGH`** rows. For each **`customer_id`**, verify Salesforce **`Account`** timelines (`Activities`, `Opportunity` renewal likelihood), Zuora **`Invoice`/`Payment`** portals or Stripe dashboards for **`severe_payment_events`**, and ServiceNow **`Incident`** counts referencing the enterprise **`account`** field — mismatches flag **`customer_id_map`** drift.

Cross-check Splunk aggregates vs **`dc(customer_id)`** expectations per pipeline stage — billing joins returning zero rows while **`logins_90d`** exceeds fifty signals mapping gaps rather than benign churn.

Peer-review weekly with actual CSM gut checks — legitimate **`HIGH`** rows should ring familiar OR expose quietly neglected portfolios (**silent churners**).

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Dashboard layout: stacked weekly **`risk_level`** counts (twelve-week **`timechart`**), rolling median **`churn_score`** trend line for **`HIGH`**, sortable **`customer_id`** grid with **`drilldown`** to **`Visualization → Timeline`** layering **`app:*`** sessions over **`snow:incident`** bars.

Attach **`splunk.singlevalue`** tile summarizing **`sum(account_arr)`** after **`lookup`** blending **`customer_id`** with CRM ARR snapshots (`| join customer_id [ search index=business sourcetype="dbx:subscriptions" | stats latest(arr) AS account_arr BY customer_id ]`).

**Splunk ITSI** (**Splunkbase 1841**, licensed separately): Glass Table tiles per CSM territory showing **`HIGH`** counts vs prior ISO week deltas.

Intervention hooks: webhook payloads include **`churn_score_base`** breakdown JSON so **Gainsight**, **Totango**, **ChurnZero**, or bespoke SuccessPlanner APIs spawn **`Save Play`** tasks referencing precise friction anchors.

Record interventions via **`collect`** into **`index=summary`** **`sourcetype=churn_intervention_log`** capturing **`timestamp`**, **`csm_owner`**, **`notes`** — thirty-day **`delta churn_score`** retrospectives fuel supervised refits.

Operational ownership sits with **Director of Customer Success Operations**, weekly steering cadence, quarterly coefficient calibration aligned with FP&A churn retrospectives.

**Step 5 — Troubleshoot**
• **`customer_id` collisions / silent misses** — Splunk surfaces **`HIGH`** scarcity despite Salesforce chaos; **`stats dc(customer_id)` by index sourcetype** reveals fifty-fold cardinality cliffs indicating unresolved merges; reload **`customer_id_map.csv`** via **`outputlookup`** automation nightly from CRM golden records.
• **HEC drops during spikes** — **`HttpInputDataHandler`** saturation yields HTTP **`503`** storms — searches **`index=_internal source=*splunkd.log* HttpInputDataHandler`** expose **`queue`** stalls; remediation expands **`maxThroughput`** / **`queueSize`**, enables **`useACK`** clientside backoff, scales ingest throughput or leverages Splunk Cloud autoscale pipelines; companion guardrail saved search compares **`eventcount`** vs trailing median (`≥30%` dip suppresses outbound digest).
• **`payment_status` grace-period drift** — Stripe/Zuora often enter **`past_due`** while autopay clears seventy-two hours later; tighten SPL predicates (**`days_past_due≥7`**) per collections policies and annotate dashboards noting **`timezone`** offsets (**GMT billing vs PST telemetry**) so ninety-day fences remain logically comparable.
• **`snow:incident` duplication** — reopen churn increments **`tickets_90d`** — optionally dedupe on **`number`**.
• **`zendesk:ticket`** translation gaps — macros referencing **`organization_id`** absent from Splunk extracts inflate **`fillnull`** zeros — adopt **`FIELDALIAS`** for **`organization_id`**.
• **`join`** overhead — **`max=0`** trades CPU for fidelity; if searches breach SLA, materialize nightly **`summary`** aggregates keyed by **`customer_id`** instead.
• **Executive skepticism** — maintain **`CSV`** reconciliation workbook tying **`churn_score`** distribution to **`NRR`** narrative.
• **`risk_decay_bonus` tuning** — oversubtracting hides dormant accounts — revert coefficients when **`HIGH`** cohort **`precision`** dips below forty percent validated.
• **`DB Connect`** JDBC interruptions — **`tail`** **`splunk_app_db_connect`** logs for **`SQLException`** credential churn — rotate passwords quietly coordinated with InfraSec calendars.
grandmaExplanation: We blend login rhythm, breadth of product use, noisy support queues, and serious billing slips — when several weaken together, customer-success teammates get a ranked heads-up before subscriptions quietly disappear.
